value.found_quote: Google has taken a layered security approach introducing security measures designed for each stage of the prompt lifecycle. From Gemini 2.5 model hardening, to purpose-built machine learning (ML) models detecting malicious instructions, to system-level safeguards, we are meaningfully elevating the difficulty, expense, and complexity faced by an attacker.
value.rationale: The page is a Google Security blog post explicitly describing Google's prompt-injection protection offering for Gemini. It details a layered defense strategy specifically targeting malicious instructions in external/tool/web content (emails, documents, calendar invites, files, URLs), including content classifiers, security thought reinforcement, markdown sanitization, suspicious URL redaction, and a user confirmation framework. These are all active defenses being rolled out, with some already deployed and others in the process of being rolled out, indicating an active lifecycle status.
